L1_COMPLETING_WHOLE.title: Lesson 1: Completing the Whole
learningGoals: Understand what Vedic Maths is and why it improves confidence.
Use base-10 completion for faster mental arithmetic.
Solve near-ten addition and subtraction with clear steps.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Introduction to Vedic Mathematics
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Multiples of Ten
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Introduction to Vedic Mathematics.
Guided: one medium question on Deficiency from Ten.
Independent: one challenge question on By Addition and By Subtraction.

screenplay.teacherLine: Namaste {{studentName}}. Welcome to Lesson 1: Completing the Whole. We start with Introduction to Vedic Mathematics and build momentum together.
screenplay.boardAction: Focus board: headline, one visual anchor, and today's key move for Introduction to Vedic Mathematics.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Introduction to Vedic Mathematics' from Lesson 1.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Introduction to Vedic Mathematics', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.teacherLine: Watch this carefully: I model Introduction to Vedic Mathematics in two clean steps, then you repeat the logic.
screenplay.boardAction: Teacher demo on board for Introduction to Vedic Mathematics, with one deliberate pause before final step.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Introduction to Vedic Mathematics' from Lesson 1.
screenplay.fallbackHint: If confused, focus on the first transformation only.

screenplay.teacherLine: Your turn now: answer by voice or text. Say the next step, not only the final answer.
screenplay.boardAction: Pause tutoring speech, keep board clean, and wait for learner input.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Introduction to Vedic Mathematics' from Lesson 1.
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student gives next step with short reasoning.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Introduction to Vedic Mathematics', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.teacherLine: Challenge beat: teach this move back to me in one sentence and solve one fresh example.
screenplay.boardAction: Show one fresh challenge prompt while preserving prior board context.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Challenge: apply Introduction to Vedic Mathematics on a new example and explain why your move works.
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student explains method and completes challenge accurately.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Use the same first move as the demo, then finish calmly.

screenplay.teacherLine: Repair beat: let us slow down and rebuild just the first mistake point together.
screenplay.boardAction: Re-draw only the failing step with color emphasis and short verbal cue.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Repair: what is the first correct move for Introduction to Vedic Mathematics?
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student states the corrected first move.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Start from the friendly base or anchor point shown on the board.

L2_DOUBLING_HALVING.title: Lesson 2: Doubling and Halving
subtopics: Extending Tables with Doubling and Halving
learningGoals: Double and halve numbers quickly without paper.
Use balancing strategies for multiplication and division.
Recognize when doubling-halving reduces effort.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Doubling by 2, 4, 8
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Extending Tables with Doubling and Halving
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Doubling by 2, 4, 8.
Guided: one medium question on Extending Tables with Doubling and Halving.
Independent: one challenge question on Dividing by 5, 50, 25.

L3_MULTIPLY_BY_11.title: Lesson 3: Digit Sum and Nine Point Circle
subtopics: Digit Sum Check for Addition and Multiplication
learningGoals: Compute digit sums rapidly and reliably.
Use digit sums for quick answer checks.
Identify recurring patterns around number nine.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Adding Digits and Digit Sums
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Casting Out Nines
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Adding Digits and Digit Sums.
Guided: one medium question on Digit Sum Check for Addition and Multiplication.
Independent: one challenge question on Number Nine Insights.

L4_VERTICAL_CROSSWISE.title: Lesson 4: Left to Right Arithmetic
subtopics: Doubling and Halving for Product Conversion
learningGoals: Perform operations from left to right with control.
Combine speed methods with correctness checks.
Handle longer numbers without losing place value.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Addition from Left to Right
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Doubling and Halving for Product Conversion
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Addition from Left to Right.
Guided: one medium question on Subtraction from Left to Right.
Independent: one challenge question on Longer Subtractions Left to Right.

L5_ALL_FROM_9_LAST_FROM_10.title: Lesson 5: All from 9 and Last from 10
learningGoals: Apply All from 9 and Last from 10 correctly.
Subtract near-base numbers in one flow.
Use the method in practical money calculations.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Applying the Formula
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Money Applications of Base Subtraction
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Applying the Formula.
Guided: one medium question on Subtraction from a Base.
Independent: one challenge question on Money Applications of Base Subtraction.

L6_NIKHILAM_BASE_10_100.title: Lesson 6: Number Splitting
learningGoals: Split hard expressions into easy mental chunks.
Use splitting across all four operations.
Keep solution flow from left to right.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Addition by Splitting
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Multiplication by Splitting
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Addition by Splitting.
Guided: one medium question on Multiplication by Splitting.
Independent: one challenge question on Left-to-Right Simplification.

learningGoals: Multiply near base numbers with minimal writing.

Extend base multiplication to larger numbers.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

L8_YAVADUNAM.title: Lesson 8: Checking and Divisibility
subtopics: First by First Last by Last Checks
learningGoals: Validate answers quickly before finalizing.
Apply divisibility tests with confidence.
Reduce avoidable arithmetic mistakes.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

screenplay.teacherLine: Namaste {{studentName}}. Welcome to Lesson 8: Checking and Divisibility. We start with Digit Sum Check for Division and build momentum together.
screenplay.boardAction: Focus board: headline, one visual anchor, and today's key move for Digit Sum Check for Division.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: give quotient and remainder logic for one quick division case in 'Digit Sum Check for Division'.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Digit Sum Check for Division', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.teacherLine: Watch this carefully: I model Digit Sum Check for Division in two clean steps, then you repeat the logic.
screenplay.boardAction: Teacher demo on board for Digit Sum Check for Division, with one deliberate pause before final step.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: give quotient and remainder logic for one quick division case in 'Digit Sum Check for Division'.
screenplay.fallbackHint: If confused, focus on the first transformation only.

screenplay.teacherLine: Your turn now: answer by voice or text. Say the next step, not only the final answer.
screenplay.boardAction: Pause tutoring speech, keep board clean, and wait for learner input.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: give quotient and remainder logic for one quick division case in 'Digit Sum Check for Division'.
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student gives next step with short reasoning.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Digit Sum Check for Division', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.teacherLine: Challenge beat: teach this move back to me in one sentence and solve one fresh example.
screenplay.boardAction: Show one fresh challenge prompt while preserving prior board context.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Challenge: apply Digit Sum Check for Division on a new example and explain why your move works.
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student explains method and completes challenge accurately.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Use the same first move as the demo, then finish calmly.

screenplay.teacherLine: Repair beat: let us slow down and rebuild just the first mistake point together.
screenplay.boardAction: Re-draw only the failing step with color emphasis and short verbal cue.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Repair: what is the first correct move for Digit Sum Check for Division?
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student states the corrected first move.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Start from the friendly base or anchor point shown on the board.

L9_GENERAL_MULTIPLICATION.title: Lesson 9: Bar Numbers
learningGoals: Convert between bar and standard forms.
Use bar numbers to simplify subtraction.
Apply bar-number thinking in multi-step sums.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Creating Bar Numbers
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

L10_DIVISION_BY_9.title: Lesson 10: Special Multiplication
subtopics: By One More than the One Before
First by First Last by Last Multiplication
learningGoals: Use special multiplication shortcuts accurately.
Select the best pattern for each number pair.
Speed up products while preserving correctness.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Multiplication by 11
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Multiplication by Nines
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Multiplication by 11.
Guided: one medium question on First by First Last by Last Multiplication.
Independent: one challenge question on Special Number Factor Spotting.

L11_VINCULUM_INTRO.title: Lesson 11: General Multiplication
learningGoals: Multiply 2-figure and 3-figure numbers systematically.
Use moving multiplier cleanly for long operands.
Connect mental and written left-to-right processes.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Revision of Core Multiplication Ideas
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Moving Multiplier for Long Numbers
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Revision of Core Multiplication Ideas.
Guided: one medium question on Three-Figure Extension.
Independent: one challenge question on Written Left-to-Right Calculations.

L12_FRACTIONS_DECIMALS.title: Lesson 12: Squaring
learningGoals: Square numbers rapidly using multiple patterns.
Verify squares using digit-sum properties.
Extend squaring methods to larger numbers.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Squaring Numbers Ending in 5
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: General Left-to-Right Squaring
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Squaring Numbers Ending in 5.
Guided: one medium question on Algebraic Squaring.
Independent: one challenge question on Squaring 3 and 4-Figure Numbers.

L13_ALGEBRAIC_IDENTITIES.title: Lesson 13: One-Line Equations
learningGoals: Solve linear equations mentally with structure.
Use inverse operations with speed and clarity.
Explain each equation step confidently.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: One-Step Equations
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Three-Step Equations
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on One-Step Equations.
Guided: one medium question on Three-Step Equations.
Independent: one challenge question on Mental One-Line Solutions.

L14_FACTORISATION.title: Lesson 14: Fractions
subtopics: Vertically and Crosswise for Fraction Addition/Subtraction
learningGoals: Add and subtract fractions using crosswise thinking.
Compare and simplify fractions quickly.
See a unified pattern across all fraction operations.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

L15_SQUARES_NEAR_BASE.title: Lesson 15: Special Division
learningGoals: Perform fast division near base numbers.
Track quotient and remainder correctly.
Adapt method for above-base and below-base divisors.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

L16_CUBES_INTRO.title: Lesson 16: The Crowning Gem (Advanced Division)
subtopics: Single Figure on the Flag Division
Negative Flag Digits with Bar Numbers
learningGoals: Use one-line division by two-figure divisors.
Control remainder strategy deliberately.
Handle advanced division with flag and bar digits.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Single Figure on the Flag Division
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Longer Number Division
workedExamples.method: Pattern + Check
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Single Figure on the Flag Division.
Guided: one medium question on Longer Number Division.
Independent: one challenge question on Decimalising the Remainder.
